targetSkillTitle: Check whether a proposed prime-factor result is complete and valid
summary: Learner can attempt factorization but misses whether all prime factors are present.

recommendedActivities.title: Verification Bridge With 12
recommendedActivities.goal: Teach the learner to check whether every factor is prime before accepting a factorization.
recommendedActivities.firstTutorQuestion: Before checking the product, are all the factors prime?

steps.title: Rewrite the Final Factorization Cleanly
steps.openingLine: Your split-work is close; now let's turn it into one clean final factorization.
steps.firstTutorQuestion: Which factors should still remain in the final line?
steps.smallHint: Keep only prime factors in the last line, and make sure none were dropped.
steps.goodStoppingPoint: Learner writes one complete prime factorization with no missing or composite factor.

steps.title: Verification Bridge With 12
steps.openingLine: Let's check whether the answer is really prime factorization, not just a matching product.
steps.firstTutorQuestion: Before checking the product, are all the factors prime?
steps.smallHint: If one factor is composite, the answer is not finished yet.
steps.goodStoppingPoint: Learner rejects 2 x 6 because 6 is not prime and accepts 2 x 2 x 3.
